title: Dubu Jorim Spicy Braised Tofu

seo.title: How to Make Dubu Jorim Spicy Braised Tofu — Easy Korean Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic Dubu Jorim Spicy Braised Tofu. This easy Korean recipe includes step-by-step instructions, tips, and serving ideas.

description: Dubu Jorim is a beloved Korean side dish featuring pan-fried tofu simmered in a savory and spicy soy-based sauce. This comforting vegetarian recipe offers a perfect balance of heat, umami, and a tender texture, making it an excellent accompaniment to any Korean meal. It is a quick and flavorful way to enjoy tofu, packed with traditional Korean flavors.

ingredients.notes: pressed and sliced into 1/2 inch thick rectangles

instructions.title: Prepare the Tofu
instructions.text: Drain the tofu block and press out excess water using paper towels or a tofu press for at least 15-20 minutes. Slice the pressed tofu into 1/2 inch thick rectangular pieces. This helps the tofu absorb flavor better and prevents it from falling apart.

instructions.title: Make the Braising Sauce
instructions.text: In a medium bowl, combine the soy sauce, gochugaru Korean chili flakes, minced garlic, minced green onion, sesame oil, and sugar. Whisk everything together until the sugar is dissolved and the sauce is well combined. Set aside.

instructions.title: Pan-Fry the Tofu
instructions.text: Heat 2 tablespoons of cooking oil in a large non-stick skillet over medium-high heat. Carefully add the sliced tofu pieces to the hot pan in a single layer. Pan-fry for 3-4 minutes per side, until golden brown and slightly crispy. Remove the tofu from the pan and set aside.

instructions.title: Simmer the Sauce with Tofu
instructions.text: In the same skillet, if there is excess oil, carefully drain some out. Pour the prepared braising sauce into the skillet. Bring the sauce to a gentle simmer. Once simmering, carefully arrange the pan-fried tofu pieces back into the skillet, ensuring they are coated with the sauce.

instructions.title: Braise the Tofu
instructions.text: Add 1/2 cup of water or vegetable broth to the skillet. Bring the mixture to a gentle boil, then reduce the heat to medium-low. Cover the skillet and let the tofu braise in the sauce for 10-15 minutes, or until the sauce has thickened slightly and the tofu has absorbed the flavors. Occasionally spoon the sauce over the tofu.

instructions.title: Garnish and Serve
instructions.text: Once the Dubu Jorim is ready, remove it from the heat. Transfer the braised tofu and sauce to a serving dish. Garnish generously with sesame seeds and an extra sprinkle of fresh sliced green onions if desired. Serve immediately as a banchan side dish or with a bowl of warm rice.

tips: For extra firm tofu, pressing out excess water is crucial. This step prevents the tofu from becoming watery and allows it to absorb the savory sauce more effectively.

Adjust the amount of gochugaru Korean chili flakes to your preferred spice level. For a milder dish, use less; for more heat, add a bit more. You can also add a pinch of black pepper for an extra kick.
Dubu Jorim tastes even better the next day as the flavors meld. Store leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3-4 days. Reheat gently on the stovetop or in the microwave.
article:
Embrace the vibrant flavors of Korean cuisine with a dish that tantalizes the taste buds and nourishes the soul: Dubu Jorim Spicy Braised Tofu. This beloved vegetarian staple, celebrated for its rich, fiery, and savory profile, transforms humble tofu into an unforgettable culinary experience. Whether you're a seasoned chef or a home cook looking to explore new horizons, mastering this recipe will open up a world of delicious possibilities. Perfect as a hearty side dish (banchan) or a light main course served with a bowl of steaming rice, Dubu Jorim Spicy Braised Tofu is a testament to the simple elegance and depth of Korean vegetarian cooking. Get ready to dive into a recipe that promises comfort, spice, and an explosion of umami.

## What is Dubu Jorim Spicy Braised Tofu?

Dubu Jorim is a classic Korean side dish where firm tofu is pan-fried to golden perfection and then gently braised in a mouth-watering, spicy, and savory sauce. The name itself offers a clue: "Dubu" means tofu, and "Jorim" refers to a cooking technique where ingredients are simmered in a seasoned broth until the liquid reduces and coats the food, infusing it with deep flavor. The result is a dish where the tofu, initially bland, absorbs the complex notes of gochugaru (Korean chili flakes), soy sauce, garlic, and often a touch of sweetness, creating a symphony of tastes and textures.

This particular rendition, Dubu Jorim Spicy Braised Tofu, emphasizes the piquant kick that is so characteristic of Korean food. It's not just about heat; it's about a balanced spiciness that complements the umami of the soy sauce and the slight sweetness of sugar or mirin. Beyond its incredible taste, Dubu Jorim is also highly regarded for its nutritional value. Tofu is an excellent source of plant-based protein, making this dish a fantastic option for vegetarians, vegans, and anyone looking to incorporate more wholesome, meat-free meals into their diet. It’s a versatile dish that embodies the heartiness and bold flavors of Korean home cooking.

## Ingredients You Need

To create your own delicious Dubu Jorim Spicy Braised Tofu, gather these essential ingredients. Freshness and quality will significantly impact the final taste.

**For the Tofu:**
*   1 block (14-16 oz / 400-450g) firm or extra-firm tofu
*   2-3 tablespoons cooking oil (vegetable, canola, or neutral oil)

**For the Braising Sauce:**
*   4 tablespoons soy sauce
*   2 tablespoons gochugaru (Korean chili flakes), adjust to your spice preference
*   1 tablespoon minced garlic (about 3-4 cloves)
*   1 tablespoon mirin (rice wine for cooking) or sake, optional (adds sweetness and depth)
*   1 tablespoon sugar or corn syrup (agave nectar or maple syrup can be substituted)
*   1 teaspoon sesame oil
*   1/2 teaspoon black pepper
*   1/2 cup water or vegetable broth
*   1/4 cup chopped green onions (for garnish and flavor)
*   1 tablespoon toasted sesame seeds (for garnish)

## Step-by-Step Instructions

Follow these steps carefully to achieve a perfectly braised and flavorful Dubu Jorim Spicy Braised Tofu.

1.  **Prepare the Tofu:** First, drain the tofu block thoroughly. Wrap it in several layers of paper towels or a clean kitchen towel and place something heavy on top (like a cutting board with a can) for at least 30 minutes to an hour. This step is crucial for removing excess water, which helps the tofu absorb flavor and fry up nicely.
2.  **Slice and Fry the Tofu:** Once pressed, unwrap the tofu and slice it into desired shapes – typically 1/2-inch thick rectangles or squares. Heat the cooking oil in a large non-stick skillet over medium-high heat. Carefully add the tofu slices, making sure not to overcrowd the pan. Fry until golden brown and slightly crispy on both sides, about 3-5 minutes per side. Remove the fried tofu from the pan and set aside on a plate lined with paper towels to absorb any excess oil.
3.  **Prepare the Braising Sauce:** While the tofu is frying or resting, combine all the braising sauce ingredients (soy sauce, gochugaru, minced garlic, mirin, sugar, sesame oil, black pepper, and water/broth) in a medium bowl. Whisk well until the sugar is dissolved and the sauce is thoroughly combined.
4.  **Braise the Tofu:** In the same skillet (you can wipe it clean if there's too much residue, but a little flavor is fine), pour in the prepared braising sauce. Bring the sauce to a gentle simmer over medium heat.
5.  **Add Tofu and Simmer:** Carefully arrange the pan-fried tofu slices in a single layer in the simmering sauce. Make sure each piece is submerged or coated. Reduce the heat to low, cover the skillet, and let the tofu braise for 10-15 minutes.
6.  **Reduce and Thicken (Optional):** After 10-15 minutes, remove the lid. If the sauce is too thin, increase the heat slightly to medium-low and continue to simmer uncovered for another 5-10 minutes, gently spooning the sauce over the tofu periodically, until the sauce has reduced and thickened to your liking, beautifully coating each piece of tofu. Be careful not to let the sauce burn.
7.  **Garnish and Serve:** Turn off the heat. Garnish the Dubu Jorim with fresh chopped green onions and a sprinkle of toasted sesame seeds. Serve immediately.

## Pro Tips for the Best Results

Achieving truly exceptional Dubu Jorim Spicy Braised Tofu involves a few insider tricks that can elevate your dish from good to extraordinary.

*   **Press Your Tofu Thoroughly:** This cannot be stressed enough. Removing as much water as possible from the tofu not only prevents splattering during frying but also creates a denser texture that is better at absorbing the delicious braising sauce. A well-pressed tofu will hold its shape and become incredibly flavorful.
*   **Don't Overcrowd the Pan:** When frying the tofu, cook it in batches if necessary. Overcrowding the pan lowers the oil temperature, leading to steamed rather than crispy tofu. Give each piece space to brown evenly.
*   **Adjust Spice to Your Liking:** Gochugaru comes in different levels of heat (mild, medium, hot). Start with the recommended amount and adjust up or down based on your personal tolerance for spice. You can always add more at the end, but it's harder to reduce the heat once it's in.
*   **Let it Rest (Briefly):** Like many braised dishes, Dubu Jorim benefits from a short resting period after cooking, allowing the flavors to meld even further. Even 5-10 minutes off the heat can make a difference.
*   **Use Quality Ingredients:** Fresh garlic, good quality soy sauce, and vibrant gochugaru will make a noticeable difference in the depth and authenticity of your dish.

## Serving Suggestions

Dubu Jorim Spicy Braised Tofu is incredibly versatile and can be enjoyed in various ways.

*   **As a Banchan (Side Dish):** This is its most traditional role. Serve it alongside a bowl of freshly cooked white or multi-grain rice and other Korean side dishes like kimchi, seasoned spinach, or bean sprouts for a complete Korean meal.
*   **With Rice as a Main:** For a simple yet satisfying meal, simply place a generous portion of Dubu Jorim over a bed of warm rice. The flavorful sauce will seep into the rice, creating a delightful bite.
*   **In a Bibimbap Bowl:** Cut the braised tofu into smaller cubes and add it as a protein component to a colorful bibimbap bowl, along with various sautéed vegetables and a fried egg.
*   **With Noodles:** While less traditional, the spicy, savory flavor of Dubu Jorim can complement simple ramen or cold noodle dishes, adding a unique texture and protein boost.
*   **Wrapped in Lettuce:** For a lighter option, wrap pieces of Dubu Jorim in fresh lettuce leaves with a dollop of ssamjang (Korean dipping sauce) for a refreshing crunch.

## Variations and Substitutions

One of the beauties of home cooking is the ability to adapt recipes to suit your taste, dietary needs, or what you have on hand.

*   **Add Vegetables:** Enhance your Dubu Jorim by adding quick-cooking vegetables during the last 5-7 minutes of braising. Sliced onions, zucchini, mushrooms, bell peppers, or even a handful of spinach or bok choy can add extra nutrients and texture.
*   **Less Spicy Version:** If you prefer less heat, reduce the amount of gochugaru or substitute some of it with paprika for color without the intense spice. You can also use gochujang (Korean chili paste) for a different kind of spiciness, but be mindful as it's often sweeter and thicker.
*   **Gluten-Free Option:** Simply use tamari instead of regular soy sauce to make this dish entirely gluten-free. Ensure all other ingredients (like gochugaru) are certified gluten-free.
*   **Sweetener Alternatives:** While sugar or corn syrup is common, you can use honey, agave nectar, or even a touch of apple juice concentrate for a more natural sweetness. Adjust the quantity according to your preference.
*   **Enhanced Umami:** For an even deeper umami flavor, consider adding a pinch of dashi powder (Japanese fish stock powder, look for vegetarian versions) or a few rehydrated shiitake mushrooms to the braising sauce.

Dubu Jorim Spicy Braised Tofu is more than just a recipe; it's an invitation to experience the comforting, bold, and wholesome essence of Korean vegetarian cuisine. With its perfect balance of heat, savoriness, and a hint of sweetness, this dish is sure to become a cherished addition to your culinary repertoire. Enjoy the process of creating this flavorful masterpiece, and savor every delicious bite!
